description_long: This object is a vintage radio receiver with a boxy, rectangular metal body covered in a weathered, rusted finish. The front panel is equipped with numerous dials, switches, and buttons, as well as a large, semi-circular frequency indicator and a rectangular glass display window. The sides feature sturdy metal handles for carrying, and the base has two metal feet for stability. The surface is heavily mottled with black and reddish-brown corrosion, giving it an aged, industrial appearance. The back and sides are mostly plain, with some panel outlines and venting visible. This radio receiver appears robust and utilitarian, likely designed for use in demanding urban or industrial environments.
description: A heavily weathered, vintage metal radio receiver with multiple dials, a frequency indicator, and sturdy side handles. Its rusted, industrial look suggests durability and age.
description_view_0: The left side view shows a plain, rusted metal surface with a prominent handle and a vented panel near the bottom.
description_view_1: The rear view displays a mostly featureless, corroded metal panel with a faint outline of an access hatch and two metal feet visible at the base.
description_view_2: The right side view reveals another plain, rusted metal surface with a subtle panel outline and no visible controls or handles.
description_view_3: The front view highlights the main control panel, featuring multiple dials, switches, a large frequency indicator, a glass display, and various labeled controls.
